sentence: You should try not to move, man.
Have to find her. Have to bring her back.

Yeah, you keep saying that. And every time I ask you who you have to bring back you pass out on me again. Since your fever's running pretty high, I'm guessing you have no idea who you're talking about.
My cuffs, my handcuffs. Where are my cuffs?
I'm not stopping. You all have a nice cookout.
Excellent, walk through the jungle in the dark.
Oooo, afraid the trees are going to get us?
No, what is knocking down the trees will get you.
Well, if you're so worried about me, how about you give me the clip back?
Put the gun back in your pants, Sawyer. Sayid's right, if you keep walking you're not going to make it to the beach.
This is Australia . This is us .

Two days ago we take off from Sydney. We fly along the same north east route every commercial airliner bound for Los Angeles does. Now the pilot, he said he lost communication with the ground, correct?
Yeah, 6 hours in. He turned around and headed for Fiji.
So, we changed course. Regrettably, no one knew we changed course. The turbulence hit. We know the rest.
The pilot said we were over a thousand miles off course.
Yeah, but, they'll find us. They have satellites in space that can take pictures of your license plate.
If only we were all wearing license plates.
Basic photography - point and shoot. Satellites can shoot, but they must be told where to point.

Okay, really enjoyed the puppet show. Fantastic. But we're stuck in the middle of damn nowhere. How about we talk about that other thing? You know that transmission Abdul picked up on his little radio? The French chick that said, "They're all dead." The transmission's been on a loop for … how long was it, Freckles?
Well, we have to tell the others when we get back.
You didn't hear anything. I'm not a stupid translator.

No one's going to tell them anything. To relay what we heard without fully understanding it will cause a panic. If we tell them what we know we take away their hope. And hope is a very dangerous thing to lose.
You say you didn't see it.
So how do you know it wasn't a dinosaur?
Oh. Yeah. So what's his story? He looks kind of... dying.
His wound is infected, but the antibiotics will fight it off.
Then his body will shut down one piece at a time. His abdomen goes rigid, then...
He looks like he's in pain.
What's this? Uh, dude? Uh... What do you think she did?
Standing guard. You heard what they said is out there.
You took my gun off me, boy?
Please, you've never even held a gun. He doesn't believe in guns. He's goes on marches.
Yeah, give it to Al Jazeera, he'll protect us.
We should give it to her.
Yeah, Kate should hold the gun.
You were sleeping in my sheep pen.
Maybe that's why I was so exhausted.
So you want to tell me why you're trespassing on my property?

Canadian. I graduated from college and figured I'd see the world. Australia was top of my list so I hopped a flight to Melbourne but I don't know anybody here so I figured I'd walk for awhile, you know.
And you just happened to wonder onto my farm.
Do you know how to work one?
My wife died 8 months ago Wednesday. She left me with too many chores and a hell of a mortgage. If you help me with the first one, I'll give you a fair wage and a place to stay.

As you and the others know, we hiked up the mountain in an attempt to help the rescue team locate us. The transceiver failed to pick up a signal. We weren't able to send out a call for help. But we're not giving up. If we gather electronic equipment - your cell phones, laptops - I can boost the signal and we can try again, but that may take some time. So for now, we should begin rationing our remaining food. If it rains, we should set up tarps to collect water. I need to organize 3 separate groups. Each group should have a leader. One group for water - I'll organize that. Who's going to organize electronics? You? Rationing food? Okay. And I believe a third group should concern themselves with the construction of...
I, um, I need to tell you something.

We couldn't send out a signal because there was another signal blocking it. The other signal - we heard it. It was a distress call from a French woman. She said that the others were dead - something had killed them all. She was alone on the Island. It's been playing for 16 years, Jack. I wanted to tell you.
How is he - the man with the shrapnel in his side?
No, just for a few seconds during the surgery.
But you told her you knew?
Well, you kind of know she's in that mug shot. And that we found those handcuffs. And that guy keeps mumbling "she's dangerous, she's dangerous" over and over.
It's not my business. Not my problem.
Yeah, you're right. We'll let Johnny Fever take care of her when he gets better.
He's not getting better if we don't get some stronger antibiotics.
That stuff I got from the luggage...
It's for ear infections and foot fungus.
What about that luggage in the overhead compartments?
But, the bodies are in there, and they're all... dead.
Look, I'll handle it. Why don't you keep an eye on him...?
Great. Yeah - love to - on it.
What are you doing in here?
Trick or treat, same as you.
Booze, smokes, couple of Playboys. What's in yours?
Well, that about sums it up, don't it?
Do you do this back home, too - steal from the dead?

Brother, you've to got wake up and smell the bull crap here, rescue ain't comin'. You're just wasting your time. You're trying to save a guy who last time I checked had a piece of metal the size of my head sticking out of his bread basket. Let me ask you something? How many of those pills are you going to use to fix him up?
Yeah? How many you got? You're just not looking at the big picture, Doc. You're still back in civilization.
Whoa, hey, I got it. I got it.
Well, look on the bright side. Whoever's this was is probably better off than we are. I got it.
여기… 당신 가방 찾은 것 같아요!
잠깐, 이리 와 봐. 니 모습 좀 봤니? 니 옷하고 얼굴이 너무 더러워 보여. 빨리 씻어라. 자기야. 사랑해.
Good. So, your husband. Was he on the flight?
I know - how modern of me.
Well, who needs men, right? Bloody useless.
So, you guys hiked all the way up that mountain for nothing, huh?
I was going to get some more water.
I was just looking for Jack and I thought he might be in there.
Yeah, no. Uh, he went to go get some medicine... over there.
What? Yeah, in the uh, uh, you know? I've got to get that water.
The bank would have given you a heck of a toaster if you had kept that in a savings account.
What do I need a toaster for?
You're hiding your wages in a tin can, Annie. I mean, I would have held on to it for you.
Weren't you going to say goodbye?

You've been here almost 3 months, Annie. And every time I ask you about yourself, you get that look in your eye. Yeah, that one. So, I mind my own business. I figured maybe you got off on the wrong side of a bad relationship. Maybe you ran. I always knew you'd leave here someday. I guess I hoped it wouldn't be in the middle of the night.
Hey, Annie, why don't you just stay one more night, eh? I'll drive you to the train station first thing in the morning. I promise.
I get it, you know. Everyone deserves a fresh start.
Just breathe. Come on, come on, look, look at me, look. What did you do?
I was just checking to if he was... he jumped on me. He grabbed me. Is he... okay?
He's not responding to antibiotics, he's bleeding internally, his fever's pushing 104. And his abdomen's rigid. He needs water.
So, what are you going to do about it?
I told you, he needs water.
No. It won't be quick. 2, 3, maybe 4 days.
Can't you put him out of his misery?
I saw your mug shot, Kate. I am not a murderer.
Do they listen to Patsy Cline in Canada?
They listen to Patsy Cline everywhere.
You sure? There's a little place up here that makes a mean burger.
What are you looking for, Ray?
Couple of days. I saw your picture in the post office. I guess they knew you were Down Under.
The reward's 23,000 dollars. I told you when I met you, I've got a hell of a mortgage. If it makes you feel any better, it was a hard decision, Annie.
Hey, who was that guy you were hanging out with?
What do you mean you don't know. What did he say?
Did he tell you not to tell me?
Mr. Locke said a miracle happened to him.
Yeah, well, a miracle happened to all of us, Walt. We survived a plane crash. Look, I don't want you hanging around with him anymore.
If you were my friend, you'd find Vincent.
Walt, look, I haven't given up on your dog. I'm going to do everything I can to find him.
I'm going to get your dog back as soon as it stops raining. Hey listen to me, I'm going to get your dog back.
Yeah, soon as it stops raining - good, nice. I'm going to find your dog... yeah, I'm just going to go walking through the haunted damn jungle looking for your... Vincent?
Oh. Something was chasing me so, uh.. I don't hear it now. But you should probably head back. Uh, I didn't see anything, you know, if you're worried about it... I didn't see anything.
I used some tribal flutes once in a recording session. I'm in a band.
I wish he would just die already.
Anything I can do to help?
The others are getting upset. They want to know what's going on inside the tent.
Came by to thank you. You going to ask what for?
For taking that gun away from me.
I didn't take it away from you.

It's sticking out of your denims, ain't it? I sure wouldn't want to be the one with that gun right now. Because everyone sitting out there listening to that poor boy scream all night knows what's got to be done. Only one that can do it is the one with that gun. Don't act so surprised. I heard you tell the hero the same thing. Hell, there's only one bullet left - be damned near poetic.
No, you need to keep quiet.
Listen to me, no matter what she does - no matter how she makes you feel, don't you trust a word that she says. She will do anything to get away.
I want to talk to her.
I want to talk to her, alone. She got to you, too, huh?
I don't know what you're talking about.
The last thing I heard before the crash - you wanted a favor.
I have one favor to ask.
Really? This ought to be good.
I wanted you to make sure that Ray Mullen got his 23 grand.
What—the guy who ratted you out?
He had a hell of a mortgage.
You really are one of a kind. You know, you would have got away if you hadn't saved him.
In case you hadn't noticed, I did get away.
You don't look free to me. Kate, I'm going to die, right?
So, are you going to do it, or what?
You let her in there alone?
What's she going to do? She's 120 pounds soaking wet.
Yeah, but she's got that gun.
She's strapped, man. I saw it in her... in her... in her... in her...
What you couldn't. Look, I get where you're coming from being a doctor and all, but he wanted it. Hell, he asked me. So, I don't like it any more than you do, but something had to be done.
You shot him in the chest?
I was aiming for his heart.
You perforated his lung. It'll take hours to bleed out.
So what is—I only had one bullet.
Vincent-I tethered him to a tree just over there. I know that Walt lost his mom. I thought that you should be the one to bring his dog back to him.
I want to tell you what I did - why he was after me.
I don't want to know. It doesn't matter, Kate, who we were - what we did before this, before the crash. It doesn't really... 3 days ago we all died. We should all be able to start over.
